source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Mad Dog Spar Rig SBM Discharge

event.top_event: Unintentional discharge of Synthetic Oil Based Mud
actions_taken: DKM turned off the pump and closed the overboard discharge valve
BSEE Inspectors conducted onsite inspection on September 15, 2017
Rig personnel replaced the leaking splitter valve as a precaution
Contractor discussed incident with all crew members in weekly safety meeting
Contractor addressed policies in place for valve line-up and valve verification
event.summary: On September 13, 2017, the Mad Dog Spar Rig unintentionally discharged 71 barrels of Synthetic Oil Based Mud (SBM) into the Gulf of Mexico due to improper valve alignment while cleaning the mud pill pit. The Derrickman and Floorhand used a valve line-up checklist to verify valve positions, but an error occurred. When the overboard discharge valve was opened and the pump activated, SBM was pumped overboard for approximately eight minutes before the pump was turned off and the valve closed. A leaking splitter valve may have been a contributing factor but was not the primary cause.
recommendations: Improve valve line-up verification procedures to prevent recurrence
Enhance training and awareness for personnel performing valve alignment tasks

hazards.name: Pressurized fluid system with overboard discharge capability
Synthetic Oil Based Mud (SBM) in active mud system

threats.name: Improper valve alignment during valve line-up
Failure to detect incorrect valve configuration before pumping

consequences.name: Discharge of 71 barrels of SBM into Gulf of Mexico

controls.name: Valve line-up checklist
controls.barrier_role: Verification of correct valve positions before fluid transfer
controls.lod_basis: Primary control to prevent incorrect valve alignment

supporting_text: the DKM and a Floorhand (FLH) used a 'valve line-up checklist' to verify that the proper valves were in the open or closed positions
BP also identified that even though a checklist was used to verify valve alignment, a mistake must have been made, and improper valve alignment did occur

controls.name: Mud Logger monitoring of active mud system
controls.barrier_role: Detection of loss of SBM in active mud system
controls.lod_basis: Secondary detection control to identify fluid loss during pumping

supporting_text: the Mud Logger contacted the DKM, Well Site Leader (WSL) & Assistant Driller (AD) to inform them of a loss of SBM in the active mud system
the Mud Logger informed him that there was a continued loss in the active system

controls.name: Pump activation and manual valve closure
controls.barrier_role: Stopping fluid discharge once loss detected
controls.lod_basis: Tertiary control to stop discharge upon detection of fluid loss

supporting_text: the DKM turned off the pump and closed the overboard discharge valve
From the time the overboard valve was initially opened to the time it was closed was approximately eight minutes

controls.name: Toolpusher authorization and key control for overboard discharge valve
controls.barrier_role: Control access to overboard discharge valve operation
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control to ensure supervisory oversight of discharge operations

supporting_text: the DKM contacted the Toolpusher (TP) and requested the key to the overboard discharge valve
the TP issued the key to the DKM to open the overboard discharge line

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
